content:
Member-only story

6 min read
Just now

--

Difficulty: Hard
Category: Graph Theory & BFS
Original Problem
Problem Summary
This is an extension of D1 (Chapter 1). Instead of a single K (number of fenceposts), we must compute the answer for all possible values of K from 1 to N.
For each K, place K fenceposts optimally to divide the fence into segments, and find the minimum time for all segments to become uniform. Sum the answers across all values of K.
If any configuration is impossible, use -1 for that K value in the sum (or skip it depending on problem statement interpretation).
Key Challenge: Efficiently iterate through all possible fencepost placements for each K without exponential blowup.
Understanding the Problem
What “All K” Means
For K = 1: Place 1 fencepost optimally; compute time For K = 2: Place 2 fenceposts optimally; compute time … For K = N: Place N fenceposts (all stakes are fenceposts); compute time
Optimal Placement
For a given K, which fencepost positions minimize the maximum segment time? This is a challenging optimization subproblem.
